purpose: Pure, props-driven presentation component for the blended welcome/invite screen (HeroAccents background, optional invite line, reused 'Just chat.' pitch, own-name input, single primary action). No transport, no card/URL decoding, no persistence — all behavior arrives via props; variant-specific content (contact vs group) is computed and supplied by the S3/S4 callers.

public_api.name: WelcomeInviteProps
public_api.signature: type WelcomeInviteProps = { inviteLine: string | null; nameValue: string; onNameChange: (value: string) => void; primaryActionLabel: string; primaryActionDisabled: boolean; onPrimaryAction: () => void; primaryActionLoading?: boolean }
public_api.description: Exported prop type — the seam contract consumed by S3 (add.tsx contact variant) and S4 (groups.tsx group variant). inviteLine is nullable: null renders no invite line (AC-CONTACT-3 fallback), enforced entirely by the caller — the component does no decode/selection logic itself. primaryActionLoading is optional (defaults to false) for an in-flight busy state on the action button; omitting it is a no-op, so it does not widen the contract for callers that don't need it.

public_api.name: WelcomeInvite
public_api.signature: export default function WelcomeInvite(props: WelcomeInviteProps): JSX.Element
public_api.description: Renders HeroAccents (existing component, reused unchanged) as the background; the invite line only when props.inviteLine !== null; the reused pitch (copy.home.subheadingLead + copy.home.subheadingPoints, not duplicated); exactly one name <Input> bound to nameValue/onNameChange (label + placeholder from copy.welcome); exactly one primary action <Button> bound to primaryActionLabel/primaryActionDisabled/onPrimaryAction/primaryActionLoading. No internal 'variant' switch — behavior is fully determined by props, not by a contact/group discriminator.

dependencies_allowed: app/src/context/LanguageContext.tsx (useCopy — existing convention, same as JoinRequestCard/HomePage)
@chakra-ui/react (Box, Heading, Text, VStack, UnorderedList, ListItem, Input, Button)
test_targets: No direct component-level unit test (no jsdom in this repo's unit setup, per architecture.md's Testing section and story notes). Verification for AC-SCREEN-1..3 is code review plus the i18n completeness check below; real behavioral proof is deferred to the S3/S4 e2e specs (add-welcome.spec.ts, groups-welcome.spec.ts).

i18n completeness: welcome.* present in Copy type + en + de (AC-I18N-1); contactInviteLine/groupInviteLine use the function-copy pattern (AC-I18N-2) — verified by reading app/src/lib/i18n.ts directly (no dedicated test file; this repo's existing *.i18n.test.ts files assert component call sites reference copy keys rather than re-asserting dictionary completeness, and this story adds no call site yet since wiring is S3/S4's job).

notes.group_primary_action_label_decision: The group-variant primary action label is NOT a new welcome.* key. copy.groups.joinRequestButton ('Request to Join' / 'Beitritt anfragen') already says the right thing and S4's story notes explicitly leave that reuse decision open ('joinRequestHeading reuse decision stays with this story only if the existing group-heading copy already says the right thing') — for the *button* label specifically, reuse is unambiguous, so S4 is expected to pass primaryActionLabel={copy.groups.joinRequestButton} directly. contactPrimaryAction ('Get started' / "Los geht's") is new because no existing copy said the right thing for the contact variant.
notes.no_variant_prop: Deliberately no `variant: 'contact' | 'group'` prop (per VQ-S2-006) — that would reintroduce a caller-specific branch into the presentation layer. S3/S4 each fully compute their own inviteLine/primaryActionLabel/onPrimaryAction and pass them in.
